content:
# Aquifer Characterization

**Summary**: The physical properties and behavior of an aquifer, including its ability to deform under stress.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2022-karami|Karami, 2022]] evaluated aquifer characterization by relating surface displacement to internal storage changes.

- [[2022-pambudi|Pambudi, 2022]] evaluated aquifer characteristics including depth, thickness, and transmissivity.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] performed aquifer-characterization to assess suitability for CO2 storage.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] performed aquifer characterization for the Khobar, Alat, and Neogene systems to assess their suitability for local use.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] performed aquifer_characterization for both the Alat and Khobar systems, including transmissivity and storativity measurements.

## Scientific Context

- [[2022-karami|Karami, 2022]] Determines how much the ground sinks when water is pumped out.

- [[2022-pambudi|Pambudi, 2022]] evaluated aquifer characteristics including depth, thickness, and transmissivity.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] performed aquifer-characterization to assess suitability for CO2 storage.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] performed aquifer characterization for the Khobar, Alat, and Neogene systems to assess their suitability for local use.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] performed aquifer_characterization for both the Alat and Khobar systems, including transmissivity and storativity measurements.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2022-karami|Karami, 2022]] Calculated using skeletal storage coefficients (Ske).

## Key Findings Across Sources

- In a confined aquifer system, the extracted groundwater estimation is derived from the value of matrix compressibility (Ske) and fluid compressibility (Ssw). ([[2022-karami|Karami, 2022, p. 34]])

- The thematic data layers including Aquifer system... were developed and utilized for the delineation of ASR potential sites. ([[2022-pambudi|Pambudi, 2022, p. 13]])

- Characterized the thickness, geometry, and geoelectric properties of the Minjur Aquifer. ([[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025, p. 18]])

- The Khobar aquifer is a calcarenitic limestone with high permeability due to fracturing; the Alat aquifer is a secondary aquifer with lower transmissivity. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 55]])

- Khobar aquifer has an average transmiss/storativity of 2.9 x 10^3 m/s and 2.1 x 10^-3 respectively. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 43]])
